title: Pickling
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Pickling process used in stainless steel manufacturing to remove oxide scale and surface impurities formed during heat treatment, resulting in a clean, corrosion-resistant surface.
learningObjectives: Identify the chemicals used during Pickling.
Understand safety requirements while handling acids.

content.introduction: During Annealing, oxide layers form on the surface of stainless steel. The Pickling process removes these oxide scales using controlled acid treatment, producing a clean and uniform surface ready for finishing operations.
content.purpose: The primary objective of Pickling is to remove oxide scale, improve surface quality, restore corrosion resistance, and prepare stainless steel for further processing or dispatch.
content.workingPrinciple: The stainless steel strip passes through acid tanks where the oxide layer reacts with the acid solution. The strip is then washed, neutralized, dried, and inspected before moving to the next process.
processSteps: Remove oxide scale using acid solution.
safetyPrecautions: Use face shield and chemical-resistant gloves.
Handle acids according to safety procedures.
Know emergency eye wash and shower locations.
content.summary: Pickling is an important surface treatment process that removes oxide scales and contaminants from stainless steel, ensuring a clean surface with excellent corrosion resistance and appearance.
